description_long: This is a vintage-style city bus with a boxy, rectangular body and a muted green and gray color scheme. The bus features large, evenly spaced rectangular windows along both sides, providing ample visibility for passengers. The front of the bus has two large windshields, a ribbed grille, and dual headlights, while the rear is simpler with ventilation grilles and tail lights. The bus rides on two axles with black wheels, and a single passenger entry door is visible on the right side, equipped with a small step for boarding. The roof is flat and slightly rounded at the edges, and the undercarriage is partially enclosed. The overall design suggests a utilitarian, mid-20th-century public transport vehicle suitable for urban environments.
description: A vintage, boxy city bus in muted green and gray with large side windows, a single entry door, and a flat roof. The bus features a classic front grille, dual headlights, and a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Rear view showing ventilation grilles, tail lights, and the flat back surface of the bus.
description_view_1: Right side view displaying the full row of large rectangular windows, the main passenger entry door with a step, and the undercarriage.
description_view_2: Front view highlighting the dual windshields, ribbed grille, and dual headlights.
description_view_3: Left side view with a continuous row of large windows and no visible entry door.
attribute_window_arrangement: Both sides feature a continuous row of six large, evenly spaced rectangular windows separated by thin vertical pillars, with all windows of similar size and shape.
